raw_response: {"rating": -5.5, "lean": "Left", "explanation": "This is an opinion/editorial piece published by the American Immigration Council, an advocacy organization. The author's own prose consistently frames the Trump administration's immigration policies in strongly negative terms, advocates for a pro-immigration worldview, and characterizes U.S. power as best used to protect human rights internationally. The density and consistency of directional language warrants a Left classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes the Trump administration's immigration vision as 'a radical departure' from traditional values, framing the administration as aberrant and destructive rather than offering a neutral description of policy differences; (2) The author uses loaded language throughout their own prose — 'fight tooth and nail,' 'pick fights,' 'bullied,' 'gleefully partnered,' 'crueler' — all of which editorialize against the administration without attribution to any source; (3) The author frames the administration's recruitment of white Afrikaner farmers as an act of racial provocation, describing it as a way 'to accuse the South African government of genocide against white people,' presenting one interpretive framing as the obvious truth; (4) The author characterizes U.S. partnerships with El Salvador and Libya as rewarding human-rights violators, a value judgment embedded entirely in the author's own connecting prose; (5) The piece presents a clear ideological premise — that American power should be used to protect human rights globally and support a nation-of-immigrants identity — which aligns directly with left-leaning values as defined by the AllSides framework; (6) The framing of 'brain drain' as a consequence of Trump policy is presented as a straightforward negative outcome with no acknowledgment of counterarguments, and the final sentence delivers an explicit moral verdict entirely in the author's own voice."}

parsed_output.explanation: This is an opinion/editorial piece published by the American Immigration Council, an advocacy organization. The author's own prose consistently frames the Trump administration's immigration policies in strongly negative terms, advocates for a pro-immigration worldview, and characterizes U.S. power as best used to protect human rights internationally. The density and consistency of directional language warrants a Left classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes the Trump administration's immigration vision as 'a radical departure' from traditional values, framing the administration as aberrant and destructive rather than offering a neutral description of policy differences; (2) The author uses loaded language throughout their own prose — 'fight tooth and nail,' 'pick fights,' 'bullied,' 'gleefully partnered,' 'crueler' — all of which editorialize against the administration without attribution to any source; (3) The author frames the administration's recruitment of white Afrikaner farmers as an act of racial provocation, describing it as a way 'to accuse the South African government of genocide against white people,' presenting one interpretive framing as the obvious truth; (4) The author characterizes U.S. partnerships with El Salvador and Libya as rewarding human-rights violators, a value judgment embedded entirely in the author's own connecting prose; (5) The piece presents a clear ideological premise — that American power should be used to protect human rights globally and support a nation-of-immigrants identity — which aligns directly with left-leaning values as defined by the AllSides framework; (6) The framing of 'brain drain' as a consequence of Trump policy is presented as a straightforward negative outcome with no acknowledgment of counterarguments, and the final sentence delivers an explicit moral verdict entirely in the author's own voice.

explanation: This is an opinion/editorial piece published by the American Immigration Council, an advocacy organization. The author's own prose consistently frames the Trump administration's immigration policies in strongly negative terms, advocates for a pro-immigration worldview, and characterizes U.S. power as best used to protect human rights internationally. The density and consistency of directional language warrants a Left classification. Detailed Analysis: (1) The author describes the Trump administration's immigration vision as 'a radical departure' from traditional values, framing the administration as aberrant and destructive rather than offering a neutral description of policy differences; (2) The author uses loaded language throughout their own prose — 'fight tooth and nail,' 'pick fights,' 'bullied,' 'gleefully partnered,' 'crueler' — all of which editorialize against the administration without attribution to any source; (3) The author frames the administration's recruitment of white Afrikaner farmers as an act of racial provocation, describing it as a way 'to accuse the South African government of genocide against white people,' presenting one interpretive framing as the obvious truth; (4) The author characterizes U.S. partnerships with El Salvador and Libya as rewarding human-rights violators, a value judgment embedded entirely in the author's own connecting prose; (5) The piece presents a clear ideological premise — that American power should be used to protect human rights globally and support a nation-of-immigrants identity — which aligns directly with left-leaning values as defined by the AllSides framework; (6) The framing of 'brain drain' as a consequence of Trump policy is presented as a straightforward negative outcome with no acknowledgment of counterarguments, and the final sentence delivers an explicit moral verdict entirely in the author's own voice.
